entry.title: Washington Post: 25 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Japan tops 104 degrees for first time in June amid record heat wave
  • World’s deepest shipwreck, the Sammy B, is discovered by explorers
  • Norway raises terror alert level after attack on eve of Pride parade
  • Ukraine accuses Russia of launching missiles from Belarusian airspace
  • U.S. abortion decision ‘horrific’ and ‘appalling,’ world leaders say
  • As election season nears, Kenyans brace for unrest and hope for peace
  • In Africa, Eastern Europe battles Russian narrative on Ukraine
  • Russia will soon exhaust its combat capabilities, Western assessments predict
  • Diplomats urge action as global food crisis deepens
  • Ukraine retreats from Severodonetsk as Russia advances in the East
  • Taliban struggles to respond to earthquake amid international isolation
  • Ukraine pulls back from Severodonetsk
  • Indigenous protesters are paralyzing Ecuador. Here’s why.
  • Is U.S. a liberal outlier on abortion, as Supreme Court opinion says?
  • Boris Johnson’s Tories suffer fresh electoral defeat; party chair quits
  • As Biden heads to Europe, the mood on Ukraine is grimmer
  • Gunman kills 2 during Oslo Pride festival; terror suspected
  • Iran and EU say Vienna nuke talks will resume in coming days
  • Egypt’s president holds talks with visiting Qatari emir
  • Russians try to block city, cement gains in eastern Ukraine
  • Deadly quake a new blow to Afghans reeling from poverty
  • Moderate quake jolts southern Iranian province, killing 1
  • Iraqi PM to head to Saudi Arabia, Iran for new dialogue
  • French lawmakers want abortion rights in constitution
  • Here we go again: Strike snarls UK trains for a third day
  • Live updates: Abortion protests expected nationwide after Supreme Court ends Roe v. Wade
  • Florida emerges as key battleground in state-by-state abortion fight
  • For these young Americans, abortion is personal — and complicated
  • GOP governors in four blue states pledge to uphold right to seek abortion
  • The scene outside the Supreme Court after Roe v. Wade was overturned
  • ‘Still a nightmare’: Surfside families mark one year since collapse
  • Pence calls for national abortion ban as Trump, GOP celebrate end of Roe
  • Biden says restoring abortion rights is up to voters
  • Oath Keepers’ defense ordered to disclose if Sidney Powell is funding attorneys
  • Susan Collins says testimony by Gorsuch, Kavanaugh ‘inconsistent’ with ruling
  • Post Politics Now: House poised to send gun legislation to Biden in response to mass shootings
  • Court temporarily halts FDA ban on Juul e-cigarettes
  • Ireland’s Abortion Battle Can Help Post-Roe America
  • Iraq’s Political Chaos Is a Win for Iran and Headache for the US
  • America’s Constitution Is Conservative, Like It or Not
  • Criminalizing Abortion Will Hurt Black Women Most
  • What are War Bonds and Why Did Ukraine Sell Them?
  • Understanding the Juul Ban and Concerns About Teen Vaping
  • The Post endorses David Blair for Montgomery County executive
  • Bolsonaro dismantled protections in the Amazon. Now, two men are dead.
  • GOP support for a gun bill offers hope for bigger reforms
  • The Supreme Court eviscerates abortion rights and its own legitimacy
  • The Supreme Court takes away a woman’s autonomy over her body
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: NY Times: 25 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Abortion Ruling Poses New Questions About How Far Supreme Court Will Go
  • In 6-to-3 Ruling, Supreme Court Ends Nearly 50 Years of Abortion Rights
  • Kavanaugh Gave Private Assurances on Roe v. Wade. Collins Says He ‘Misled’ Her.
  • Century-Old State Laws Could Determine Where Abortion Is Legal

entry.content:

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 25 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 25 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-25 14:00PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Business chambers in Hong Kong urge Beijing’s liaison office to help push for removal of Covid-19 quarantine measures to revive ailing economy
  • Hong Kong’s homebuyers snap up Grand Jete’s new flats in Tuen Mun to get ahead of rising mortgage rates
  • Hong Kong’s largest public healthcare workers union opts to dissolve as journalism group lowers vote threshold for disbandment
  • Hong Kong national security police arrest 2 more suspected to be linked to possible bomb plots involving 30kg of chemicals

  • Hongkongers all: drawn to the city for different reasons, they call it home
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong hotels raise rates for quarantine rooms as number of arrivals to city outpaces available spots

  • How’s the China-India economic relationship changed 2 years on from the deadly Galwan Valley border clash?
  • Chinese engineering firm builds Bangladesh longest bridge - but paid for by nation

  • China’s live-streaming e-commerce offers lifeline to fruit farmers amid Covid restrictions and short season
  • A bridge too far? Taipei mayor draws fire for proposal to link Quemoy and Xiamen
  • China jobs: facing lean times, small businesses dial back summer hiring
  • China ‘no-limits’ vow with Russia raises Pentagon urgency to prepare for Guam attack: US commander
  • USS Samuel B Roberts: world’s deepest shipwreck found in the Philippine Sea
  • Why is a South Korean fringe group backing Japan’s position on WWII ‘comfort women’?
  • US, Japan and allies form new Pacific group to blunt China’s influence
  • Philippines’ Duterte leaves brutal legacy in presidency marked by drug war, US-China policy flip-flops
  • 6 arrested in Indonesia for blasphemy after bar’s free drinks for Muhammad promo backfires
  • Ukraine war: our troops have ‘almost left’, says Sievierodonetsk mayor
  • Morocco says 18 migrants died during attempted crossing of 2,000 into Spanish enclave, with ‘dozens piled on the ground’
  • Roe vs Wade: protesters condemn ‘tragedy’ as several states impose new abortion bans
  • Norway probes ‘Islamist terror’ in Oslo shooting hours before Pride march, with 2 killed, 21 injured
  • Roe vs Wade: How betting on Trump paid off for US anti-abortion supporters
  • Call reveals Russia’s Putin told Macron he ‘wanted to play ice hockey’ on eve of Ukraine invasion
  • Roe vs Wade: Judge in US abortion ruling signals gay marriage, contraception next in firing line
  • Mexico woman, 22, dies climbing off-limits active volcano
  • Friends at first sniff: people drawn to others who smell like them, study shows
  • US passes first major gun control bill in decades
  • Ukraine war highlights G7’s role as geopolitical linchpin
  • Sunac China may miss instalment payment on 4 billion yuan bond as lockdowns pile pressure on beleaguered Chinese developer
  • US pop band Backstreet Boys evokes Chinese nostalgia with 44 million tuning in for concert on Tencent’s WeChat
  • Julie Otsuka writes about dementia with vision and wisdom in The Swimmers, but its dazzling opening jars with what follows
  • Hong Kong art project spreads message of unity and positivity on 25th handover anniversary, with sculptures and multimedia installations
  • Polygenic risk scores: lowering a child’s odds of disease, or a slippery slope to designer babies?
  • Chef Samuel Lee Sum on being a Hongkonger in Paris, passing a test ‘worse than Top Chef or MasterChef’, and where to eat in the French capital
  • Kingdom star Ju Ji-hoon eyed for new sci-fi drama Dominant Species; Cho Seung-woo set to play pianist turned lawyer in Sacred Divorce – K-drama casting latest
  • Netflix Korean reality show Change Days, hosted by comedian Jang Do-yeon, gets couples to break up with each other
  • How aromatherapy can benefit your mind and body – and how essential oils help with cancer patients’ recovery
  • A US bakery tried to trademark mochi muffins and failed – rightfully. Not every food creation needs to be trademarked
  • Keep your NFT collection on your smartwatch with Tag Heuer’s new Connected Calibre E4 – now you can show off your crypto art investments on your wrist
  • 5 child stars who survived financial meltdowns: from Britney Spears’ 13-year conservatorship to Lindsay Lohan’s rehab stint and Macaulay Culkin’s trust fund
  • Inside Elon Musk and first wife Justine Wilson’s murky marriage: their trans daughter Vivian Jenna Wilson just ditched his name for her mum’s, but what was their pre-fame relationship really like?
  • Meet James Wilkie Broderick, son of Sarah Jessica Parker and Matthew Broderick – from his SATC fashion icon mum’s down-to-earth parenting style to his possible future music career
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 25 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Heat records set as scores in Tokyo treated for heatstroke
  • Japan’s top court orders Twitter to delete tweet on man’s past arrest
  • Teen pair get mayor to set aside 100 million yen to upgrade facilities
  • Hibakusha hail first meeting of TPNW as a major step forward
  • More companies with ties to Toyota targeted in cyberattacks
  • China’s Xi to visit Hong Kong for handover anniversary
  • Yellen to visit South Korea to discuss sanctions against North
  • UN office points to Israel in Al Jazeera reporter's death
  • Russian missiles rain down on military sites across Ukraine
  • US abortion ruling sparks global debate, polarizes activists
  • Ukrainian army leaving battered city for fortified positions
  • Supreme Court overturns Roe v. Wade; states can ban abortion
  • Study: For good health, don’t stay up later than 2 hours on days off
  • EDITORIAL: Onus on nuclear powers to work with nuclear ban treaty members
  • VOX POPULI: Recovery of missing flash drive simply a very lucky break
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: Korea Herald: 25 Jun, 2022

Articles in this issue:

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 25 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • In 6-to-3 Ruling, Supreme Court Ends Nearly 50 Years of Abortion Rights
  • Abortion Ruling Poses New Questions About How Far Supreme Court Will Go
  • Americans Celebrate and Mourn Abortion Decision as New Fights Loom
  • ‘It’s Become Real’: Abortion Decision Roils Midterms, Sending Fight to States

  • Companies Are More Vocal Than Ever on Social Issues. Not on Abortion.

  • States Rush to Revamp Laws After Supreme Court’s Gun Ruling

  • U.S. Outlines Compensation Rules for Some Havana Syndrome Victims

  • The Next Battlefield for N.Y. Progressives? The State Assembly.
  • A Monkeypox Vaccine Is Now Available for New Yorkers Who May Be at Risk
  • Harry Gesner, Architect of Soaring California Style, Dies at 97
